summary: Add overlay toggle control (wind/rain/temp) to map view

acceptance_criteria_satisfied.description: User taps 'Rain' toggle -> Rain becomes selected and polyline colors switch to rain-based
acceptance_criteria_satisfied.details: OverlayToggle component uses ToggleGroup with single-selection mode. Rain toggle button triggers onValueChange callback with 'rain' value.

acceptance_criteria_satisfied.description: Route has wind data but no rain data -> Rain toggle is disabled with visual indication
acceptance_criteria_satisfied.details: OverlayToggle accepts availability prop. Rain toggle shows disabled state (opacity: 0.4, gray icon) when availability.rain is false.

acceptance_criteria_satisfied.description: Only one overlay active at a time (single-select mode)
acceptance_criteria_satisfied.details: ToggleGroup type='single' ensures only one value is active. Previous selection automatically deselects.

acceptance_criteria_satisfied.description: No route selected -> Overlay toggle is hidden
acceptance_criteria_satisfied.details: Home screen conditionally renders OverlayToggle only when selectedOption exists (line 348).

patterns_followed: Used existing ToggleGroup component from components/ui/toggle-group.tsx
Defaults to 'wind' overlay when route is first selected (line 122-124)
Positioned above map controls (zIndex: 25 vs controls zIndex: 30)
Disabled toggles with visual indication (opacity: 0.4, muted colors)
implementation.semantic_theme_usage: Uses useSemanticTheme hook throughout. All colors, spacing, and radius values from semantic tokens.

tests.note: Unit tests not implemented yet. Storybook stories cover all acceptance criteria scenarios.

typecheck.note: Pre-existing TypeScript errors in codebase (not related to US-003 implementation). The overlay-toggle.tsx file itself has no type errors.

lint.note: Pre-existing ESLint configuration issue (missing react-native plugin). Not related to US-003 implementation.
